market_signals.summary: Anthropic launched a $1.5B standalone enterprise AI services joint venture with Blackstone, Hellman & Friedman, and Goldman Sachs (plus Apollo, General Atlantic, GIC, Leonard Green, Sequoia as partners). The structure mirrors Palantir's forward-deployment model — Anthropic engineers embedded directly in the venture — and is explicitly designed to undercut traditional consulting firms by combining Claude access with implementation capability. Anthropic also launched the Claude Partner Network (March 2026) with $100M committed for partner training, co-marketing, and joint market development.

OpenAI launched 'The Development Company,' a $10B enterprise AI services venture backed by 19 investors, running parallel to Anthropic's JV. Announced on the same day as Anthropic's venture. OpenAI closed a $122B funding round at $852B post-money valuation in March 2026 and is generating ~$2B/month in revenue (enterprise >40% of mix).

ElevenLabs tripled its valuation to $11B as of early May 2026. The company is one of the fastest-growing AI platforms, with voice synthesis expanding into enterprise and developer markets. At $11B it is now a significant late-stage target. No open CoS or S&O postings surfaced this week.

Modal Labs (AI inference/compute infrastructure) is in talks to raise at a $2.5B valuation, more than doubling its $1.1B Series B valuation from late 2025. Revenue hit $50M ARR in February 2026. A Head of Product Marketing role is open on Ashby. Industry fit for Tyler is moderate (dev tools/infra) but the trajectory and stage are notable.

Harvey (legal AI, ~$11B valuation) hired Keith Enright (former Google CPO, ex-Gibson Dunn) as Chief Strategy Officer in March 2026. CSO seat is now filled, but Harvey's rapid growth and legal-AI category leadership may generate CoS or S&O roles soon. Worth monitoring on a monthly basis.

Cursor hired Brian McCarthy as President of Global Revenue and Field Operations (February 2026). Cursor is at ~$500M–1B ARR and was valued at $29B. With a President of Revenue now in seat, organizational growth may create CoS or Head of Strategic Operations openings in H1 2026. Worth monitoring.

Anthropic's CPO Mike Krieger resigned from Figma's board in April 2026 after reports that Anthropic is building a competing design/AI product. Signals Anthropic is moving aggressively into new product categories — a leading indicator of commercialization and GTM hiring.

summary.note: No roles cleared the selective filter this week. The most actionable signal is the simultaneous Anthropic + OpenAI enterprise JV announcements (May 4): both are building Palantir-style commercialization arms that will need GTM/commercialization/CoS leadership — roles that do not yet exist as open postings. Tyler's Converge experience (product commercialization inside a $150M professional services platform) is unusually strong positioning for these JVs when hiring opens. Worth monitoring Anthropic careers and the new JV entity over the next 30–60 days. ElevenLabs ($11B) and Cursor ($29B) are also scaling fast enough that senior ops/strategy roles are likely to surface in coming weeks.
summary.selective_mode_rationale: Tyler landed Converge Commercialization Lead in Q1 2026. The bar to surface a role is: exceptional target company (OpenAI/Anthropic/Perplexity/Figma/Linear/Oura/Substack/Airbnb tier), $200K+ base minimum, AND a credible sentence for why it's worth leaving a brand-new product role at Deloitte Converge. Roles reviewed this week (EliseAI CoS $175–250K/NYC, Handshake CoS posted March, Modal HoPMM/infra-focus, Fathom HoPMM/$91M stage) did not meet the combined threshold.
